description: Phonetics and exclusion rules for Fonoran root assignment. Semantic concepts live in data/fonoran-concept-inventory.json.

philosophy.premise: Invariant concept roots; grammar via particles; compounds are transparent trees.
philosophy.phonetic_strategy: Huffman-like: higher fundamentality → lower phonetic cost (shorter, simpler syllables).

concept_source.note: The embedded concepts[] list was removed. All generators read data/fonoran-concept-inventory.json.

reserved_particles.note: Grammar particles, never lexical roots (docs/fonoran-grammar.md Rule 3). Full inventory + roles in data/fonoran-grammar-particles.json. Active v1 particles: mi=pronoun I, ta=past, sa=future, no=negation, ya=affirmation, von=if. REMOVED FROM GRAMMAR in v1 but kept reserved (blocked from lexical reuse for spelling stability, pending a future decision): wo (question marker) and the v*/z* forms that were interrogatives (vus/zas/zes/zis/zos/zus) and focus (vat/vet/vit). v1 questions are compositional + '?' with no question particle. Spatial relations and other pronouns are lexical, not particles. (Future is sa, not na, to keep it phonemically distinct from the negation particle no.)

excluded_syllables.note: Awkward or humorous; deferred to later expansion.

phonetics.note: r and j onsets are excluded from primitive assignment (constitution rule 1). See tools/fonoran-phonetic-weights.js isBannedPrimitiveSpelling().
